message:
feat(vdbe): improve vectorized morsel dispatch with e2e validation artifacts

Refine the VDBE vectorized dispatch implementation and add end-to-end
validation for morsel-based batch processing:

vectorized_dispatch.rs:
- Restructure dispatch logic for clearer morsel boundary handling
- Improve error propagation and batch completion signaling
- Better separation of fast-path vs fallback dispatch paths

New artifacts:
- artifacts/bd-1rw.2/morsel_dispatch_e2e_artifact.json: structured
  evidence from morsel dispatch e2e validation run
- scripts/bd_1rw_2_morsel_dispatch_e2e.sh: reproducible e2e script
  for morsel dispatch validation (bead bd-1rw.2)

Also updates vectorized_batch_format.md documentation and beads issue
tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -272,7 +272,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1r3d","title":"EPIC: RaptorQ decode proofs + explainability artifacts","description":"Implement spec expectations around decode failure explainability.

Spec essentials:
- When decoding fails, system produces explainable artifacts (DecodeProof / failure reasons).
- Proof artifacts must be replay-verifiable.

Epic closes when:
- decoder emits proof objects on failure;
- proof verification tooling exists;
- failure reasons are exposed in deterministic logs/reports.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:37:26.380964458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T22:51:21.122826009Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T22:51:21.122801764Z","close_reason":"All decode-proof implementation and verification lanes are closed; parent epic complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-1d2i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-221l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-3go","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-awqq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-faz4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1863,"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Decode Proofs (Explainable Repair)

### Thesis
Repair without explanation is a correctness liability. Decode proofs make repairs auditable:
- what bytes did we trust?
- what did we reconstruct?
- why do we believe reconstruction is correct?

### Production vs Lab
- Production may keep proof emission off for performance.
- Lab/harness must enable proofs so failures are debuggable and regressions are attributable.

### Required Properties
- proof schema versioned
- verifier exists and catches mismatches
- proof does not leak raw page bytes by default (hashes + metadata)","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:00:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw","title":"Epic: VDBE Execution Engine","description":"Virtual Database Engine: 190+ opcodes, register-based VM, cursor management, sorter, aggregate engine. Morsel-driven parallelism (§8.2) for OLAP. Cranelift JIT compilation path for hot queries (§8.3).

## Success Criteria
Epic completion requires all child beads to satisfy their structured acceptance criteria, including explicit unit/property coverage, deterministic e2e evidence, and structured logging/metrics validation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every child bead in this epic defines concrete unit/property test scope with deterministic pass/fail thresholds and invariant IDs. 2. Critical-path end-to-end scenarios are implemented as reproducible e2e scripts (fixed seeds, replay commands, artifact bundles) across happy-path and adversarial-path behavior. 3. Structured logging and metrics contracts are mandatory for all child outputs (trace_id, run_id, scenario_id, seed, timing, outcome, error context) and validated by CI conformance checks. 4. Epic completion requires a machine-readable evidence manifest linking child unit/e2e/log artifacts to acceptance outcomes, plus an explicit fallback/rollback plan that preserves concurrent-writer defaults.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:57:49.310465041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:07.932247942Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","epic","ev-gated","frankensqlite","vdbe"]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw.1","title":"Register-based VM with 190+ opcodes","description":"Register-based VM: 190+ opcodes, program counter, register file, cursor array. Opcode dispatch (threaded code or computed goto). Tracing: span 'vdbe_exec' with fields opcode_count, program_id, result_rows. Log level: TRACE per-opcode, DEBUG per-statement, INFO for slow queries (>100ms). Metric: fsqlite_vdbe_opcodes_executed_total counter, fsqlite_vdbe_statement_duration_seconds histogram.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 190+ SQLite opcodes implemented and tested. 2. Register-based dispatch < 10ns per opcode. 3. Correct results on full sqllogictest suite. 4. Memory usage bounded by configured limits.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:08.878795609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T08:27:57.066370982Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T08:27:57.066341316Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: 190 opcodes defined (152 fully implemented, 38 advanced/optional stubs), program counter with fetch-execute loop, register file (1-indexed Vec<SqliteValue>), cursor array (MemCursor/SorterCursor/StorageCursor), match-based opcode dispatch, vdbe_exec tracing span with opcode_count/program_id/result_rows fields, TRACE-level per-opcode logging, DEBUG-level per-statement logging, INFO-level slow query logging (>100ms), fsqlite_vdbe_opcodes_executed_total counter, fsqlite_vdbe_statement_duration_seconds histogram approximation via cumulative microsecond counter.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1rw.2","title":"Morsel-driven parallel execution engine","description":"Morsel-driven parallelism (§8.2): Partition work into cache-sized morsels, schedule across worker threads. Implements exchange operators (hash partition, broadcast). Pipeline breakers at sort/aggregate boundaries. Tracing: span 'morsel_exec' with fields morsel_size, worker_id, pipeline_id. Log level: DEBUG morsel scheduling, INFO pipeline completion. Metric: fsqlite_morsel_throughput_rows_per_sec gauge, fsqlite_morsel_workers_active gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Near-linear scaling to 4 cores on TPC-H Q1. 2. Deterministic results regardless of thread count. 3. Morsel size auto-tuned to L2 cache. 4. Exchange operators handle skewed data correctly.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:08.929279534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:47:09.135784260Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3028,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed via bv robot triage as highest-impact unclaimed actionable task. Added unit tests for auto-tuning coverage and gauge updates; updated vectorized_dispatch benchmark to use auto-tuned partitioning; updated docs/vectorized_batch_format.md. Validation: rch exec cargo fmt --check PASS; rch exec cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch PASS (6 tests). rch clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -D warnings blocked by pre-existing clippy error in crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swizzle.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:38:20Z"},{"id":3032,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resuming bd-1rw.2 as next concrete high-impact implementation bead after closing bd-n0g4q. Scheduler now uses hash_partition_exchange assignments (with explicit schedule_strategy='hash_exchange' tracing) instead of pure NUMA round-robin for this pipeline kind, with configuration mismatch guardrail. Added test hash_join_probe_scheduling_uses_hash_exchange_strategy to verify balanced worker distribution while preserving task completion. Validation this slice: cargo fmt --all PASS; rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch -- --nocapture PASS (11 tests). rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -- -D warnings remains blocked by pre-existing unrelated clippy errors in crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/unix.rs (useless_conversion).","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:44:51Z"},{"id":3037,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up: synced docs/vectorized_batch_format.md to reflect runtime behavior ( uses  scheduling for all-HashJoinProbe task sets; other kinds remain NUMA round-robin). Reserved and then released  + docs paths via Agent Mail to keep edit ownership explicit.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:47:03Z"},{"id":3038,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction: docs sync completed in docs/vectorized_batch_format.md.  {"id":"bd-1rw.3","title":"Cranelift JIT compilation for hot queries","description":"Cranelift JIT (§8.3): Compile hot query plans to native code via Cranelift. Tiered: interpret first, JIT after N executions. Code cache with LRU eviction. Fallback to interpreter on compilation failure. Tracing: span 'jit_compile' with fields plan_hash, compile_time_us, code_size_bytes. Log level: INFO JIT trigger, WARN compilation failure with fallback. Metric: fsqlite_jit_compilations_total counter, fsqlite_jit_cache_hit_ratio gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. JIT'd queries produce identical results to interpreter. 2. Compilation < 10ms for typical queries. 3. 10x throughput improvement on hot OLAP queries. 4. Graceful fallback to interpreter on any compilation failure.  8. Unit/property test coverage for this bead is mandatory, including deterministic seeds and explicit invariant checks. 9. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script must validate integrated behavior with replay instructions and archived artifacts. 10. Structured logging/metrics validation is required: trace/run/scenario IDs, timing, counters, and actionable failure context must be present and CI-verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:08.983068401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:13.605975523Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw.4","title":"Cursor management, external sorter & aggregate engine","description":"Cursor & sorter: B-tree cursor with seek/next/prev, external merge sort for ORDER BY/GROUP BY with memory budget and spill-to-disk. Aggregate accumulator engine. Tracing: span 'sort' with fields rows_sorted, spill_pages, merge_runs. Log level: WARN on disk spill, DEBUG sort progress. Metric: fsqlite_sort_spill_pages_total counter, fsqlite_sort_rows_total counter.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. External sort handles datasets > available memory. 2. Spill-to-disk with < 2x write amplification. 3. Merge sort produces correct ordered output. 4. Aggregate engine handles NULL and DISTINCT correctly.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:09.035037588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:06.798410630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3ta.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw.5","title":"Vectorized column-at-a-time execution with SIMD","description":"Vectorized execution (§8.2): Column-at-a-time processing within morsels. SIMD-accelerated filter/project/hash. Selection vectors for predicate evaluation. Integrates with morsel scheduling. Tracing: span 'vectorized_batch' with fields batch_size, selectivity, simd_path. Log level: DEBUG batch processing stats. Metric: fsqlite_vectorized_rows_total counter, fsqlite_vectorized_simd_utilization gauge.

@@ -0,0 +1,116 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# bd_1rw_2_morsel_dispatch_e2e.sh — deterministic verifier for bd-1rw.2
+#
+# Verifies morsel-dispatch deterministic behavior and captures an artifact with:
+# - run_id / trace_id / scenario_id
+# - per-worker-count measurements
+# - replay command for deterministic reruns
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+WORKSPACE_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+
+JSON_OUTPUT=false
+NO_RCH=false
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --json)
+      JSON_OUTPUT=true
+      shift
+      ;;
+    --no-rch)
+      NO_RCH=true
+      shift
+      ;;
+    *)
+      echo "ERROR: unknown argument '$1'" >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+BEAD_ID="bd-1rw.2"
+SCENARIO_ID="${SCENARIO_ID:-VDBE-1}"
+SEED="${SEED:-424242}"
+TRACE_ID="${TRACE_ID:-$SEED}"
+RUN_ID="${RUN_ID:-${BEAD_ID}-seed-${SEED}}"
+MIN_SPEEDUP="${MIN_SPEEDUP:-0.60}"
+
+ARTIFACT_DIR="$WORKSPACE_ROOT/artifacts/bd-1rw.2"
+ARTIFACT_PATH="${FSQLITE_MORSEL_E2E_ARTIFACT:-$ARTIFACT_DIR/morsel_dispatch_e2e_artifact.json}"
+mkdir -p "$(dirname "$ARTIFACT_PATH")"
+
+export RUN_ID TRACE_ID SCENARIO_ID SEED
+export FSQLITE_MORSEL_E2E_ARTIFACT="$ARTIFACT_PATH"
+
+TEST_NAME="vectorized_dispatch::tests::morsel_dispatch_e2e_replay_emits_artifact"
+if ! $NO_RCH && command -v rch >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe "$TEST_NAME" -- --exact --nocapture
+else
+  cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe "$TEST_NAME" -- --exact --nocapture
+fi
+
+if [[ ! -f "$ARTIFACT_PATH" ]]; then
+  echo "ERROR: expected artifact at '$ARTIFACT_PATH'" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  echo "ERROR: jq is required for artifact validation" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+jq -e --arg bead "$BEAD_ID" '.bead_id == $bead' "$ARTIFACT_PATH" >/dev/null
+jq -e --arg run_id "$RUN_ID" '.run_id == $run_id' "$ARTIFACT_PATH" >/dev/null
+jq -e --arg scenario_id "$SCENARIO_ID" '.scenario_id == $scenario_id' "$ARTIFACT_PATH" >/dev/null
+jq -e --argjson trace_id "$TRACE_ID" '.trace_id == $trace_id' "$ARTIFACT_PATH" >/dev/null
+jq -e '.deterministic_checksum == true' "$ARTIFACT_PATH" >/dev/null
+jq -e '.measurements | length == 3' "$ARTIFACT_PATH" >/dev/null
+
+BASE_TPS="$(jq -r '.measurements[] | select(.worker_threads == 1) | .throughput_tasks_per_sec' "$ARTIFACT_PATH")"
+FOUR_TPS="$(jq -r '.measurements[] | select(.worker_threads == 4) | .throughput_tasks_per_sec' "$ARTIFACT_PATH")"
+
+if [[ -z "$BASE_TPS" || -z "$FOUR_TPS" ]]; then
+  echo "ERROR: missing worker_threads=1 or worker_threads=4 measurements" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+SPEEDUP="$(awk -v base="$BASE_TPS" -v four="$FOUR_TPS" 'BEGIN { if (base <= 0) { print "0.000"; } else { printf "%.3f", four / base; } }')"
+if ! awk -v speedup="$SPEEDUP" -v floor="$MIN_SPEEDUP" 'BEGIN { exit !(speedup + 0 >= floor + 0) }'; then
+  echo "ERROR: speedup ${SPEEDUP}x is below threshold ${MIN_SPEEDUP}x" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+REPLAY_CMD="$(jq -r '.replay_command' "$ARTIFACT_PATH")"
+
+if $JSON_OUTPUT; then
+  cat <<ENDJSON
+{
+  "bead_id": "$BEAD_ID",
+  "run_id": "$RUN_ID",
+  "trace_id": $TRACE_ID,
+  "scenario_id": "$SCENARIO_ID",
+  "seed": $SEED,
+  "artifact_path": "${ARTIFACT_PATH#$WORKSPACE_ROOT/}",
+  "base_throughput_tasks_per_sec": $BASE_TPS,
+  "four_worker_throughput_tasks_per_sec": $FOUR_TPS,
+  "speedup_x": $SPEEDUP,
+  "min_speedup_x": $MIN_SPEEDUP,
+  "replay_command": "$REPLAY_CMD"
+}
+ENDJSON
+else
+  echo "=== Morsel Dispatch E2E Verification ==="
+  echo "Bead ID:                    $BEAD_ID"
+  echo "Run ID:                     $RUN_ID"
+  echo "Trace ID:                   $TRACE_ID"
+  echo "Scenario ID:                $SCENARIO_ID"
+  echo "Seed:                       $SEED"
+  echo "Artifact:                   ${ARTIFACT_PATH#$WORKSPACE_ROOT/}"
+  echo "Throughput (1 worker):      $BASE_TPS tasks/sec"
+  echo "Throughput (4 workers):     $FOUR_TPS tasks/sec"
+  echo "Speedup (4/1):              ${SPEEDUP}x (threshold ${MIN_SPEEDUP}x)"
+  echo "Replay command:"
+  echo "  $REPLAY_CMD"
+fi
